recommended_action: Keep California usable for staging, but treat 1GB as a production-risk floor. Prefer a 2GB or 4GB upgrade, or keep heavier factory jobs off-host until capacity is increased.
notes: Swap is present and actively used, which helps staging survive but confirms memory pressure exists.
Review-watch is low-risk to keep on California, but the full commercial payment stack should not be called production-ready at 1GB without explicit acceptance.
If California stays at 1GB, formal cutover should require explicit user acknowledgement of the low-memory risk before DNS is changed.
